title: Rodrigues
common: The surname Rodrigues does not have a unique origin, because it is a patronymic meaning "Rodrigo's son". This complexity conducts Américo de Moura to list 18 different Rodrigues' families (<htcite0>).

families.name: Rodrigues (Velho) - São Vicente/São Paulo
history: All data we have about this family is present in Brazil (<htcite0>), and these documents do not bring any information about his origin.

people.name: António Rodrigues
history:
João M. de Almeida resumes the documentation we have and, as consequence what we know about António Rodrigues (), below it is listed the sources used by João M. de Almeida:
  • He owns land near a Port named Naus ().
  • In his work about São Vicente, Marcos A. Lança cites the António Rodrigues' shipyard ().
  • The work about Pero Lopes de Sousa's diary also has a citation about António Rodrigues residence being near a Port ().
  • The Friar Gaspar Madre de Deus informs us that Martim Afonso de Sousa gave the lands near to Tumiaru to António Rodrigues, he also give us information about a "porto de Tumiaru" and gave even more details about the São Vicente region ().

When we talk about his family, we do not have documents to prove the relationships, but Priest Jorge Moreira wrote some memories saying "... que com João Ramalho veio Antonio Rodrigues, o qual, diz o author, casara com uma filha de Piquerobi Cacique da aldêa de Hururay." (). Américo de Moura worked with this family, informing that Luiz G. Silva Leme discovered, and published, a manuscript from Captain Diogo Gonçalves Moreira (), these data can be found in the First Volume of Luiz G. Silva Leme's work, where he calls attention that they are contradictory with Pedro Taques' suggestion (). In the next generation, I describe how genetic helps us to confirm that at least one of his [possible] daughters are in fact indigenous.

marriages.name: Unknown / Antonia Rodrigues
history: According to Jorge Moreira's memories (<htcite0>).

children.name: Garcia Rodrigues
history: According to Diogo Gonçalves Moreira's notes, that could be extracted from Padre Jorge Moreira's memories (<htcite0>), these notes were published by Luis G. Silva Leme (<htcite1>).

source.text: Silva Leme, Luiz G., 1904, Volume I

children.name: Isabel Velho

source.text: Silva Leme, Luiz G., 1904, Volume I
Silva Leme, Luiz G., 1904, Volume I

people.name: Garcia Rodrigues
history: Américo de Moura describes him affirming "Considero Garcia Rodrigues vicentino e casado na terra, não sei se uma ou mais vezes, nem com quem..." [I consider Garcia Rodrigues was born in San Vicente, and he married here, I do not know with who neither how many times ...] (), and genetic sequencing of her [possible] daughters show that they cannot have the same mother. The relationship with his hypothetical father is not documented, and we have as the unique reference some genealogical notes letters ().

Américo de Moura described what we know about his life, so we only list the sources that confirm them:
  • There are no documents from Santo André da Borda do Campo, but it is still possible to check data from those days through Tito Livio Ferreira's work ();
  • His actions in São Paulo Town Hall are listed in "Actas" ();
  • It is also possible to find information in "Registro Geral da Câmara de São Paulo" ().
  • A document from Santo André was published in Informativo Arquivo Municipal ();

marriages.name: Isabel Velho
history: According to Pedro T de A. Paes Leme (<htcite0>) and Luiz G. Silva Leme (<htcite1>), probably because the unique Garcia Rodrigues' daughter that we have documentation was named as Isabel Velho.

source.text: Paes Leme, Pedro T. de A., 1976, Tomo III
Silva Leme, Luiz G., 1904, Volume I

marriages.name: Unknown
history: According to Américo Moura (<htcite0>).
The genetic sequence of mitochondrial DNA (<htcite1>) of [possible] descendants proves she has a mitochondrial haplogroup B2 (<htcite2> ; <htcite3>).

children.name: Mecia Rodrigues
history: Américo de Moura (<htcite0>) and Luiz G. Silva Leme's hypothesis (<htcite1>), but Pedro T. de A. Paes Leme did not follow them (<htcite2>).
She has a mitochondrial haplogroup different from Isabel Velho (<htcite3> ; <htcite4>), this means they cannot be sisters with the same parents.

children.name: Isabel Rodrigues
history: Pedro T. de A. Paes Leme does not cite this hypothesis (<htcite0>) and Américo de Moura rejects it (<htcite1>), but Luiz G. Silva Leme suggests it (<htcite2>).
The genetic sequence of her descendants confirms she has the same Mécia Rodrigues' mitochondrial haplogroup (<htcite3> ; <htcite4>).

Both Jorge Moreira and Antonio Bicudo, her husband, worked together without any impediment in Town Hall (<htcite5>), so it is necessary to consider that either she is not Garcia Rodrigues' daughter (<htcite6>), or some laws were too flexible to allow brothers-in-law to work together when only mothers were different (<htcite7>).

source.text: Atas Da Câmara da Cidade de São Paulo, 1562-1596
Atas Da Câmara da Cidade de São Paulo, 1562-1596
Registro Geral da Câmara de São Paulo, Volume VII
100 anos do Arquivo Histórico Municipal: um olhar sobre o precioso acervo

people.name: Isabel Velho
history: It was not possible to find a unique document that proves her existence (<htcite0>), but Pedro Taques says that he could see, and this document informs that she and her husband came from Porto - PT (<htcite1>).

The mitochondrial haplogroup B2 (<htcite2> ; <htcite3> ; <htcite4> ; <htcite5>) does not allow considering her an Antonia Rodrigues' daughter, because she had mitochondrial haplogroup A2-T16111C (<htcite6> ; <htcite7>), but this result does not remove the possibility of Antonio Rodrigues to be her father.

Diogo Luiz de A. P. de Vasconcellos cites Isabel Velho as a descendant of Maria Alvares Cabral, a hypothetical sister of Pedro Alvares Cabral (<htcite8>). The book does not give additional information about the primary source that sustains the affirmation, so it is not possible to validate it. But, a researched made by Visconde de Sanches Baéna shows this information is not correct (<htcite9>). This hypothesis is also incompatible with her mitochondrial DNA (<htcite10>).
Her supposed generation is shown in his supposed brother and husband.

marriages.name: Garcia Rodrigues

people.name: Mécia Rodrigues
history: The connection wih her parents is an Américo de Moura (<htcite0>) and Luiz G. Silva Leme's hypothesis (<htcite1>), that is a different understanding of Pedro T. de A. Paes Leme (<htcite2>).
She had a mitocondrial haplogroup different of Isabel Velho (<htcite3> ; <htcite4>), so she cannot be her sister, but she still can be her half-sister.

marriages.name: Domingos Gonçalves da Maia
history: It is accepted that this marriage happened, because Luiz G. Silva Leme reports that he had a testament in 1627 "com testamento em 1627" (), so I suppose that he had access to it, but there was a wrong association with a hypothetical son Garcia Rodrigues (Velho), husband of Catarina Dias (), como descreve-se abaixo.

source.text: Silva Leme, Luiz G., 1904, Volume VI
Silva Leme, Luiz G., 1904, Volume VII

people.name: Garcia Rodrigues (Velho)
history:
We have two different hypotheses for Garcia Rodrigues' parents, so we show how to use the scientific method () to select them:
  • The scientific method starts with the hypotheses:
    • HYPOTHESIS 1: Luiz G. Silva Leme informs that Garcia Rodrigues would be son of Domingos Gonçalves da Maia, "com testamento em 1627" (), and Mécia Rodrigues, and he had marriage with Catarina Dias ().
    • HYPOTHESIS 2: Américo de Moura suggests that he is the son of Garcia Rodrigues and a one of his wives, he had marriage with Catarina Dias ().
  • After having the hypotheses, it is necessary to look for primary sources that allow to confirm them:
    • HYPOTHESIS 1: it was not possible to find any document to confirm it, so when Luiz G. Silva Leme had access to his supposed father's testament, he could see his name there ()
    • HYPOTHESIS 2: in "Cartas e Datas de São Paulo", we are informed there "Anno de Nascimento do Nosso Senhor Jesús Cristo de mil quinhentos e noventa e quatro... Garcia Rodrigues e bem assim sua mulher Catharina Dias ... Tabatinguara que ficaram a elle dito Garcia Rodrigues e a seus irmãos por morte e falecimento de seu pae e mãe delle" [The year of birth of Our Lord Jesus Christ, one thousand five hundred and ninety-four... Garcia Rodrigues and his wife Catharina Dias... Tabatinguara who were left behind by Garcia Rodrigues and his brothers upon the death and death of their father and his mother] (). The Antônio Rodrigues' inventory cites a Garcia Rodrigues, but the document does not inform his wife's name (), but in a document selling some "casas" it is informed that "no Anno de Nascimento de Nosso Senhor Jesus Cristo de mil quinhentos e noventa e quatro... Antonio Rodrigues, filho de Garcia Rodrigues, defunto morador nesta dita villa.." [in the year of birth of Our Lord Jesus Christ, one thousand five hundred and ninety-four... Antonio Rodrigues, son of Garcia Rodrigues, deceased resident of this vilage...] ().
  • The THEORY is made using primary sources and comparing them with the hypotheses.
    • A HYPOTHESIS 1 informs us that Garcia Rodrigues Velho's supposed father was still alive in the year "1627" and the same hypothesis does not list Antônio Rodrigues as his brother, so we can arrive at the initial conclusion that we are talking about homonymous, but we cannot accept any information given as source of truth, so we need to study the HYPOTHESIS 2. Antônio Rodrigues says in his inventory that he had a brother named Garcia Rodrigues. During sells that happened in the year 1594, there was a declaration of both that their parents passed away, and Antônio Rodrigues added that his father's name was Garcia Rodrigues. Additional information, the wife's name, appear in a sell made by Garcia Rodrigues, forcing us to discard momentarily Luiz G. Silva Leme's hypothesis. Q.E.D. (Util new documents appear that can change current understanding, because science always advances with data.)

people.name: Garcia Rodrigues Velho
history:
The captain Garcia Rodrigues Velho, as her wife's uncle, André Fernandes, is one of the people that Camargo's family hated, because he helped Pires family

"favoreceu aos Pires"

during the war (). The hate against him by Camargo's family resulted in two defamation (): the first used his wife, that is Fernandes' descendant, while the second said that he did not become a Priest to be a Jewish person, but in reallity he did not become a Priest because he killed people and made other crimes ( "homicídios e outros delitos").

people.name: Maria Garcia
history: She had defamation without any basis of being a Jewish person (<htcite0>), this event had relationship with the hate against her father, and the brother of her grandmother during the civil war (<htcite1>). It is possible to find more details about defamation in her mother text (<htcite2>).
In a research not finalized, it was discovered that her mitochondrial haplogroup is C1b (subgroup) (<htcite3> ; <htcite4>).
Her generation is listed in her husband family.

marriages.name: Fernão Dias Paes
history: According to her grandson's diligence, that has their son baptism (<htcite0>).

source.text: Torre do Tombo, Tribunal do Santo Ofício: Conselho Geral, Habilitações, Pedro, mç. 25, doc. 476

people.name: Isabel Rodrigues
history: There was no conclusion about her ascendants (; ), but her son Antonio Bicudo declared in his inventory that "sou filho de Antonio Bicudo natural da Ilha de São Miguel e de Izabel Rodrigues natural desta terra" (our emphasis) [I am the son of Antonio Bicudo, a native of the Island of São Miguel and of Izabel Rodrigues, a native of this land] [I am son of Antonio Bicudo from São Miguel island and Izabel Rodrigues that was born here] (), and the genetic, allow us to arrive to conclusion. Before the genetic sequence to be discovered, both hypothesis had the same baseline. With the popularization of these tests, the Amarilis Paiva Almeida's son, that would be a [possible] direct descendant of Garcia Rodrigues' [possible] wife, got a complete genetic sequence of his genetic mitochondrial, that was published on YFull ( ; ), in this genetic sequence, the mitochondrial genome is given from mother to her descendants. According to Pedro T. de A. Paes Leme's hypothesis, that informs the supposed Isabel Velho came from Porto, the expectation would be to have a mitochondrial genome from Europe. The molecular data of a [possible] Garcia Rodrigues' descendant shows that her origin is Amerindian, and this is not possible in Europe in the 16th century. The genetic sequence of Amarilis Paiva Almeida's son is exactly the same shown by Luiz Gustavo de Sillos for Isabel Rodrigues' descendants ().
On 18th November 1598, because her husband, Antonio Bicudo, was not present ("ausente"), she requested land for São Paulo Town Hall (<htcite6>).
Her generation will be exposed in her husband's family, but it is necessary to finish some documentation collection to clarify some details.

marriages.name: Antonio Bicudo
history: According to her son inventory (<htcite0>) and document available in São Paulo Town Hall (<htcite1>).

source.text: Registro Geral da Câmara de São Paulo, 1919, Volume 7
Registro Geral da Câmara de São Paulo, 1919, Volume 7

people.name: Isabel Velho
history:
Pedro T. de A. Paes Leme suggested she was born in Porto like her parents (), but genetic sequence does not allow this showing that her mother is an Indigenous (), so at least her mother is not a European, but this was not the unique doubt about her, and we use scientific method () to select her possible father:
  • The scientific method () starts with the hypotheses:
    • HYPOTHESIS 1: according to Eugênio de A. da Cunha e Freitas, she would be Paulo Teixeira's daughter ().
    • HYPOTHESIS 2: is accepted by the majority of researchers. According to it, she would be Garcia Rodrigues' daughter ().
  • After having the hypotheses, it is necessary to look for primary sources that allow to confirm them:
    • HYPOTHESIS 1: there is a "prazo" of Vimieiro that talks about the family ().
    • HYPOTHESIS 2: her ascendance through Rodrigues is proved in São Paulo records (). Information available in the canonization document of Priest Saint José de Anchieta enforces this hypothesis, because Maria Moreira informs "Maria Moreira (ouvida a 20 de julho de 1627), natural de São Paulo, com cerca de 60 anos de idade, filha de Jorge Moreira e de Isabel Velho" ().
  • The THEORY is made using primary sources and comparing them with the hypotheses:
    • A HYPOTHESIS 1 uses a "prazo" in Portugal, that authors published different information:
      • The information initially shown informs that Paulo Teixeira's wife is not known, and the author lists 5 children for Rui Teixeira and Isabel Velha. He also adds that Rui Teixeira passed away in 1538.
      • Maurício Antonino Fernandes shows a different data, as Manoel Abranches de Soveral shown in his work (), according to him, Melícia Teixeira, Tomaz Fernandes' wife, generated Rodrigo Teixeira, Frutuoso Teixeira, and Paulo Teixeira, so Melícia Teixeira is not her sister. Manuel Abranches de Soveral informs that in Sande, on 21st February 1593, Paulo Teixeira and Maria Marques had marriage.
      • On Geneall web page, it is possible to observe that José Huet de Bacellar transcribes a part of Prof. Souza Lara's book, where it is confirmed the marriage date, but there is divergence about the parents (). On the web page is also added that the daughter Isabel Velha had marriage with Jorge Moreira.

      The HYPOTHESIS 2 works only with São Paulo documentation. With this available data, it is possible to say that we have a homonymous couple, because if it is accepted that Maria Moreira is a daughter of listed parents by HYPOTHESIS 1, in 1627 we would have a daughter 26 years older than her mother, and this is not possible. Q.E.D. (Util new documents appear that can change current understanding, because science always advances with data.)
Her generation will be described with her husband's family.

marriages.name: Jorge Moreira
history: According to canonization of Saint José de Anchieta (<htcite0>).

source.text: Paes Leme, Pedro T. de A., 1976, Tomo III
da Cunha e Freitas, Eugênio de A., 1951
